revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site | name = Sandenkanaya Kofun | native_name = 散田金谷古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Ishikawa Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Sandenkanaya Kofun | map_size = | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|51|20|N|136|48|58|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Hōdatsushimizu, Ishikawa]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Hokuriku region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (park) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }}
The {{nihongo|'''Sandenkanaya Kofun'''|散田金谷古墳|}}} is a late [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial tumulus]] located in the Sanden neighborhood of the town of [[Hōdatsushimizu, Ishikawa]] in the [[Hokuriku region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1982.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138417/9 |title=散田金谷古墳|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Sandenkanaya Kofun is located a hill at the confluence of the Shio River and the Kose River at the base of the [[Noto Peninsula]]. The tumulus is an oval-shaped {{nihongo|''empun''-style|円墳}} ''kofun'', with maximal length of 21 meters, partially surrounded by horseshoe-shaped moat three meters wide and 0.5 meters deep. The horizontal [[burial chamber]] opens to the southwest, and has a total length of 9.85 meters, length of 5.80 meters, width of 2.65 meters, and height of 2.76 meters and is the largest in the Hokuriku region. It is made of [[granite]] and [[andesite]] and the sarcophagus is made of granite. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
*Major axis: 21meters *Minor axis: 18.5 meters *Height: 3.5 meters
This tomb was excavated by locals in 1903, and a house-shaped [[sarcophagus]] with a roofline decorated with [[Chigi (architecture)|''chigi'']] was found. It is an Ishikawa Prefectural Tangible Cultural Property. [[Grave goods]] included [[Sue ware]], swords, [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirrors]], beads and other jewellery, and horse fittings. The tumulus is believed to have been built in the latter half of the 6th century, based on excavated items.<ref name="Shiseki"/> Restoration work on the tumulus was carried out from 1986 to 1988, and the tumulus was partially dismantled and repaired in 2006 to 2007.
Some of the excavated items are kept at the [[Tokyo National Museum]]. The site is open to the public as part of an [[archaeological park]]. The burial chamber can be viewed only from the outside. It is located about 20 minutes by car from [[Minami-Hakui Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Nanao Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ishikawa)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[http://www.pref.ishikawa.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunkazai/siseki/1-5.html Ishikawa Prefectural information home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[http://www.hodatsushimizu.jp/kanko/spot/detail.jsp?id=5486&genre=1 Hōdatsushimizu Information] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.hot-ishikawa.jp/spot/5491Ishikawa Tourist Information] {{in lang|ja}}

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Sandenkanaya Kofun | native_name = 散田金谷古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Ishikawa Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Sandenkanaya Kofun | map_size = | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|51|20|N|136|48|58|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Hōdatsushimizu, Ishikawa]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Hokuriku region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (park) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }}
The {{nihongo|'''Sandenkanaya Kofun'''|散田金谷古墳|}}} is a late [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial tumulus]] located in the Sanden neighborhood of the town of [[Hōdatsushimizu, Ishikawa]] in the [[Hokuriku region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1982.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138417/9 |title=散田金谷古墳|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Sandenkanaya Kofun is located a hill at the confluence of the Shio River and the Kose River at the base of the [[Noto Peninsula]]. The tumulus is an oval-shaped {{nihongo|''empun''-style|円墳}} ''kofun'', with maximal length of 21 meters, partially surrounded by horseshoe-shaped moat three meters wide and 0.5 meters deep. The horizontal [[burial chamber]] opens to the southwest, and has a total length of 9.85 meters, length of 5.80 meters, width of 2.65 meters, and height of 2.76 meters and is the largest in the Hokuriku region. It is made of [[granite]] and [[andesite]] and the sarcophagus is made of granite. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
*Major axis: 21meters *Minor axis: 18.5 meters *Height: 3.5 meters
This tomb was excavated by locals in 1903, and a house-shaped [[sarcophagus]] with a roofline decorated with [[Chigi (architecture)|''chigi'']] was found. It is an Ishikawa Prefectural Tangible Cultural Property. [[Grave goods]] included [[Sue ware]], swords, [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirrors]], beads and other jewellery, and horse fittings. The tumulus is believed to have been built in the latter half of the 6th century, based on excavated items.<ref name="Shiseki"/> Restoration work on the tumulus was carried out from 1986 to 1988, and the tumulus was partially dismantled and repaired in 2006 to 2007.
Some of the excavated items are kept at the [[Tokyo National Museum]]. The site is open to the public as part of an [[archaeological park]]. The burial chamber can be viewed only from the outside. It is located about 20 minutes by car from [[Minami-Hakui Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Nanao Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ishikawa)]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site | name = Sandenkanaya Kofun | native_name = 散田金谷古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Ishikawa Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Sandenkanaya Kofun | map_size = | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|51|20|N|136|48|58|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Hōdatsushimizu, Ishikawa]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Hokuriku region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (park) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }}
The {{nihongo|'''Sandenkanaya Kofun'''|散田金谷古墳|}}} is a late [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial tumulus]] located in the Sanden neighborhood of the town of [[Hōdatsushimizu, Ishikawa]] in the [[Hokuriku region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1982.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138417/9 |title=散田金谷古墳|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Sandenkanaya Kofun is located a hill at the confluence of the Shio River and the Kose River at the base of the [[Noto Peninsula]]. The tumulus is an oval-shaped {{nihongo|''empun''-style|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} ''kofun'', with maximal length of 21 meters, partially surrounded by horseshoe-shaped moat three meters wide and 0.5 meters deep. The horizontal [[burial chamber]] opens to the southwest, and has a total length of 9.85 meters, length of 5.80 meters, width of 2.65 meters, and height of 2.76 meters and is the largest in the Hokuriku region. It is made of [[granite]] and [[andesite]] and the sarcophagus is made of granite. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
*Major axis: 21meters *Minor axis: 18.5 meters *Height: 3.5 meters
This tomb was excavated by locals in 1903, and a house-shaped [[sarcophagus]] with a roofline decorated with [[Chigi (architecture)|''chigi'']] was found. It is an Ishikawa Prefectural Tangible Cultural Property. [[Grave goods]] included [[Sue pottery]], swords, [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirrors]], beads and other jewellery, and horse fittings. The tumulus is believed to have been built in the latter half of the 6th century, based on excavated items.<ref name="Shiseki"/> Restoration work on the tumulus was carried out from 1986 to 1988, and the tumulus was partially dismantled and repaired in 2006 to 2007.
Some of the excavated items are kept at the [[Tokyo National Museum]]. The site is open to the public as part of an [[archaeological park]]. The burial chamber can be viewed only from the outside. It is located about 20 minutes by car from [[Minami-Hakui Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Nanao Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ishikawa)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[http://www.pref.ishikawa.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunkazai/siseki/1-5.html Ishikawa Prefectural information home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[http://www.hodatsushimizu.jp/kanko/spot/detail.jsp?id=5486&genre=1 Hōdatsushimizu Information] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.hot-ishikawa.jp/spot/5491Ishikawa Tourist Information] {{in lang|ja}}

It is made of [[granite]] and [[andesite]] and the sarcophagus is made of granite. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
*Major axis: 21meters *Minor axis: 18.5 meters *Height: 3.5 meters
This tomb was excavated by locals in 1903, and a house-shaped [[sarcophagus]] with a roofline decorated with [[Chigi (architecture)|''chigi'']] was found. It is an Ishikawa Prefectural Tangible Cultural Property. [[Grave goods]] included [[Sue pottery]], swords, [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirrors]], beads and other jewellery, and horse fittings. The tumulus is believed to have been built in the latter half of the 6th century, based on excavated items.<ref name="Shiseki"/> Restoration work on the tumulus was carried out from 1986 to 1988, and the tumulus was partially dismantled and repaired in 2006 to 2007.
Some of the excavated items are kept at the [[Tokyo National Museum]]. The site is open to the public as part of an [[archaeological park]]. The burial chamber can be viewed only from the outside. It is located about 20 minutes by car from [[Minami-Hakui Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Nanao Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

Some of the excavated items are kept at the [[Tokyo National Museum]]. The site is open to the public as part of an [[archaeological park]]. The burial chamber can be viewed only from the outside. revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site | name = Sandenkanaya Kofun | native_name = 散田金谷古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Ishikawa Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Sandenkanaya Kofun | map_size = | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|51|20|N|136|48|58|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Hōdatsushimizu, Ishikawa]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Hokuriku region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (park) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }}
The {{nihongo|'''Sandenkanaya Kofun'''|散田金谷古墳|}}} is a late [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial tumulus]] located in the Sanden neighborhood of the town of [[Hōdatsushimizu, Ishikawa]] in the [[Hokuriku region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1982.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138417/9 |title=散田金谷古墳|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Sandenkanaya Kofun is located a hill at the confluence of the Shio River and the Kose River at the base of the [[Noto Peninsula]]. The tumulus is an oval-shaped {{nihongo|''empun''-style|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} ''kofun'', with maximal length of 21 meters, partially surrounded by horseshoe-shaped moat three meters wide and 0.5 meters deep. The horizontal [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] opens to the southwest, and has a total length of 9.85 meters, length of 5.80 meters, width of 2.65 meters, and height of 2.76 meters and is the largest in the Hokuriku region. It is made of [[:en:Granite|Granite]] and [[:en:Andesite|Andesite]] and the sarcophagus is made of granite. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
*Major axis: 21meters *Minor axis: 18.5 meters *Height: 3.5 meters
This tomb was excavated by locals in 1903, and a house-shaped [[sarcophagus]] with a roofline decorated with [[Chigi (architecture)|''chigi'']] was found. It is an Ishikawa Prefectural Tangible Cultural Property. [[Grave goods]] included [[Sue pottery]], swords, [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirrors]], beads and other jewellery, and horse fittings. The tumulus is believed to have been built in the latter half of the 6th century, based on excavated items.<ref name="Shiseki"/> Restoration work on the tumulus was carried out from 1986 to 1988, and the tumulus was partially dismantled and repaired in 2006 to 2007.
Some of the excavated items are kept at the [[Tokyo National Museum]]. The site is open to the public as part of an [[archaeological park]]. The burial chamber can be viewed only from the outside. It is located about 20 minutes by car from [[Minami-Hakui Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Nanao Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ishikawa)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[http://www.pref.ishikawa.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunkazai/siseki/1-5.html Ishikawa Prefectural information home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[http://www.hodatsushimizu.jp/kanko/spot/detail.jsp?id=5486&genre=1 Hōdatsushimizu Information] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.hot-ishikawa.jp/spot/5491Ishikawa Tourist Information] {{in lang|ja}}

It is located about 20 minutes by car from [[Minami-Hakui Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[Nanao Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

The {{nihongo|'''Sandenkanaya Kofun'''|散田金谷古墳|}}} is a late [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial tumulus]] located in the Sanden neighborhood of the town of [[:en:Hōdatsushimizu, Ishikawa|Hōdatsushimizu, Ishikawa]] in the [[:en:Hokuriku region|Hokuriku region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1982.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138417/9 |title=散田金谷古墳|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Sandenkanaya Kofun is located a hill at the confluence of the Shio River and the Kose River at the base of the [[Noto Peninsula]]. The tumulus is an oval-shaped {{nihongo|''empun''-style|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} ''kofun'', with maximal length of 21 meters, partially surrounded by horseshoe-shaped moat three meters wide and 0.5 meters deep. The horizontal [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] opens to the southwest, and has a total length of 9.85 meters, length of 5.80 meters, width of 2.65 meters, and height of 2.76 meters and is the largest in the Hokuriku region. It is an Ishikawa Prefectural Tangible Cultural Property. The tumulus is believed to have been built in the latter half of the 6th century, based on excavated items.<ref name="Shiseki"/> Restoration work on the tumulus was carried out from 1986 to 1988, and the tumulus was partially dismantled and repaired in 2006 to 2007.
Some of the excavated items are kept at the [[Tokyo National Museum]]. The site is open to the public as part of an [[:en:Archaeological park|Archaeological park]]. The burial chamber can be viewed only from the outside. It is located about 20 minutes by car from [[Minami-Hakui Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[Nanao Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Sandenkanaya Kofun | native_name = 散田金谷古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Ishikawa Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Sandenkanaya Kofun | map_size = | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|51|20|N|136|48|58|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Hōdatsushimizu, Ishikawa|Hōdatsushimizu, Ishikawa]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[:en:Hokuriku region|Hokuriku region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (park) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }}
The {{nihongo|'''Sandenkanaya Kofun'''|散田金谷古墳|}}} is a late [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial tumulus]] located in the Sanden neighborhood of the town of [[:en:Hōdatsushimizu, Ishikawa|Hōdatsushimizu, Ishikawa]] in the [[:en:Hokuriku region|Hokuriku region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1982.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138417/9 |title=散田金谷古墳|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Sandenkanaya Kofun is located a hill at the confluence of the Shio River and the Kose River at the base of the [[Noto Peninsula]]. The tumulus is an oval-shaped {{nihongo|''empun''-style|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} ''kofun'', with maximal length of 21 meters, partially surrounded by horseshoe-shaped moat three meters wide and 0.5 meters deep. The horizontal [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] opens to the southwest, and has a total length of 9.85 meters, length of 5.80 meters, width of 2.65 meters, and height of 2.76 meters and is the largest in the Hokuriku region. It is made of [[:en:Granite|Granite]] and [[:en:Andesite|Andesite]] and the sarcophagus is made of granite. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
*Major axis: 21meters *Minor axis: 18.5 meters *Height: 3.5 meters
This tomb was excavated by locals in 1903, and a house-shaped [[sarcophagus]] with a roofline decorated with [[:en:Chigi (architecture)|''chigi'']] was found. It is an Ishikawa Prefectural Tangible Cultural Property. [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] included [[Sue pottery]], swords, [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirrors]], beads and other jewellery, and horse fittings. The tumulus is believed to have been built in the latter half of the 6th century, based on excavated items.<ref name="Shiseki"/> Restoration work on the tumulus was carried out from 1986 to 1988, and the tumulus was partially dismantled and repaired in 2006 to 2007.
Some of the excavated items are kept at the [[Tokyo National Museum]]. The site is open to the public as part of an [[:en:Archaeological park|Archaeological park]]. The burial chamber can be viewed only from the outside. It is located about 20 minutes by car from [[:en:Minami-Hakui Station|Minami-Hakui Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Nanao Line|Nanao Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ishikawa)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ishikawa)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[http://www.pref.ishikawa.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunkazai/siseki/1-5.html Ishikawa Prefectural information home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[http://www.hodatsushimizu.jp/kanko/spot/detail.jsp?id=5486&genre=1 Hōdatsushimizu Information] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.hot-ishikawa.jp/spot/5491Ishikawa Tourist Information] {{in lang|ja}}

[[Category:Completion tier 4]] }}

==Overview== The Sandenkanaya Kofun is located a hill at the confluence of the Shio River and the Kose River at the base of the [[:en:Noto Peninsula|Noto Peninsula]]. The tumulus is an oval-shaped {{nihongo|''empun''-style|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} ''kofun'', with maximal length of 21 meters, partially surrounded by horseshoe-shaped moat three meters wide and 0.5 meters deep. The horizontal [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] opens to the southwest, and has a total length of 9.85 meters, length of 5.80 meters, width of 2.65 meters, and height of 2.76 meters and is the largest in the Hokuriku region. It is made of [[:en:Granite|Granite]] and [[:en:Andesite|Andesite]] and the sarcophagus is made of granite. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
*Major axis: 21meters *Minor axis: 18.5 meters *Height: 3.5 meters
This tomb was excavated by locals in 1903, and a house-shaped [[:en:Sarcophagus|Sarcophagus]] with a roofline decorated with [[:en:Chigi (architecture)|''chigi'']] was found. It is an Ishikawa Prefectural Tangible Cultural Property. [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] included [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], swords, [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirrors]], beads and other jewellery, and horse fittings. The tumulus is believed to have been built in the latter half of the 6th century, based on excavated items.<ref name="Shiseki"/> Restoration work on the tumulus was carried out from 1986 to 1988, and the tumulus was partially dismantled and repaired in 2006 to 2007.
Some of the excavated items are kept at the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]]. The site is open to the public as part of an [[:en:Archaeological park|Archaeological park]]. The burial chamber can be viewed only from the outside. It is located about 20 minutes by car from [[:en:Minami-Hakui Station|Minami-Hakui Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Nanao Line|Nanao Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ishikawa)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ishikawa)]]
==References== {{reflist}}

The {{nihongo|'''Sandenkanaya Kofun'''|散田金谷古墳|}}} is a late [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial tumulus]] located in the Sanden neighborhood of the town of [[:en:Hōdatsushimizu, Ishikawa|Hōdatsushimizu, Ishikawa]] in the [[:en:Hokuriku region|Hokuriku region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1982.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138417/9 |title=散田金谷古墳|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Sandenkanaya Kofun is located a hill at the confluence of the Shio River and the Kose River at the base of the [[:en:Noto Peninsula|Noto Peninsula]]. The tumulus is an oval-shaped {{nihongo|''empun''-style|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} ''kofun'', with maximal length of 21 meters, partially surrounded by horseshoe-shaped moat three meters wide and 0.5 meters deep. The horizontal [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] opens to the southwest, and has a total length of 9.85 meters, length of 5.80 meters, width of 2.65 meters, and height of 2.76 meters and is the largest in the Hokuriku region. It is made of [[:en:Granite|Granite]] and [[:en:Andesite|Andesite]] and the sarcophagus is made of granite. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
*Major axis: 21meters *Minor axis: 18.5 meters *Height: 3.5 meters
This tomb was excavated by locals in 1903, and a house-shaped [[:en:Sarcophagus|Sarcophagus]] with a roofline decorated with [[:en:Chigi (architecture)|''chigi'']] was found. It is an Ishikawa Prefectural Tangible Cultural Property. [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] included [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], swords, [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirrors]], beads and other jewellery, and horse fittings. The tumulus is believed to have been built in the latter half of the 6th century, based on excavated items.<ref name="Shiseki"/> Restoration work on the tumulus was carried out from 1986 to 1988, and the tumulus was partially dismantled and repaired in 2006 to 2007.
Some of the excavated items are kept at the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]]. The site is open to the public as part of an [[:en:Archaeological park|Archaeological park]]. The burial chamber can be viewed only from the outside. It is located about 20 minutes by car from [[:en:Minami-Hakui Station|Minami-Hakui Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Nanao Line|Nanao Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ishikawa)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ishikawa)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[http://www.pref.ishikawa.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunkazai/siseki/1-5.html Ishikawa Prefectural information home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[http://www.hodatsushimizu.jp/kanko/spot/detail.jsp?id=5486&genre=1 Hōdatsushimizu Information] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.hot-ishikawa.jp/spot/5491Ishikawa Tourist Information] {{in lang|ja}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Sandenkanaya Kofun]][[ja:散田金谷古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q18336996]] Wikidata name: Sandenkanaya Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:05, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Sandenkanaya Kofun | native_name = 散田金谷古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Ishikawa Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Sandenkanaya Kofun | map_size = | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|36.855306|N|136.816028|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Hodatsushimizu|WD=Q1204817}} | region = [[:en:Hokuriku region|Hokuriku region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (park) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }}
The {{nihongo|'''Sandenkanaya Kofun'''|散田金谷古墳|}}} is a late [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial tumulus]] located in the Sanden neighborhood of the town of [[:en:Hōdatsushimizu, Ishikawa|Hōdatsushimizu, Ishikawa]] in the [[:en:Hokuriku region|Hokuriku region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1982.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138417/9 |title=散田金谷古墳|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Sandenkanaya Kofun is located a hill at the confluence of the Shio River and the Kose River at the base of the [[:en:Noto Peninsula|Noto Peninsula]]. The tumulus is an oval-shaped {{nihongo|''empun''-style|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} ''kofun'', with maximal length of 21 meters, partially surrounded by horseshoe-shaped moat three meters wide and 0.5 meters deep. The horizontal [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] opens to the southwest, and has a total length of 9.85 meters, length of 5.80 meters, width of 2.65 meters, and height of 2.76 meters and is the largest in the Hokuriku region. It is made of [[:en:Granite|Granite]] and [[:en:Andesite|Andesite]] and the sarcophagus is made of granite. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
*Major axis: 21meters *Minor axis: 18.5 meters *Height: 3.5 meters
This tomb was excavated by locals in 1903, and a house-shaped [[:en:Sarcophagus|Sarcophagus]] with a roofline decorated with [[:en:Chigi (architecture)|''chigi'']] was found. It is an Ishikawa Prefectural Tangible Cultural Property. [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] included [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], swords, [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirrors]], beads and other jewellery, and horse fittings. The tumulus is believed to have been built in the latter half of the 6th century, based on excavated items.<ref name="Shiseki"/> Restoration work on the tumulus was carried out from 1986 to 1988, and the tumulus was partially dismantled and repaired in 2006 to 2007.
Some of the excavated items are kept at the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]]. The site is open to the public as part of an [[:en:Archaeological park|Archaeological park]]. The burial chamber can be viewed only from the outside. It is located about 20 minutes by car from [[:en:Minami-Hakui Station|Minami-Hakui Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Nanao Line|Nanao Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ishikawa)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ishikawa)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[http://www.pref.ishikawa.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunkazai/siseki/1-5.html Ishikawa Prefectural information home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[http://www.hodatsushimizu.jp/kanko/spot/detail.jsp?id=5486&genre=1 Hōdatsushimizu Information] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.hot-ishikawa.jp/spot/5491Ishikawa Tourist Information] {{in lang|ja}}

It is located about 20 minutes by car from [[:en:Minami-Hakui Station|Minami-Hakui Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Nanao Line|Nanao Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

The {{nihongo|'''Sandenkanaya Kofun'''|散田金谷古墳|}}} is a late [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial tumulus]] located in the Sanden neighborhood of the town of [[:en:Hōdatsushimizu, Ishikawa|Hōdatsushimizu, Ishikawa]] in the [[:en:Hokuriku region|Hokuriku region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1982.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138417/9 |title=散田金谷古墳|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Sandenkanaya Kofun is located a hill at the confluence of the Shio River and the Kose River at the base of the [[:en:Noto Peninsula|Noto Peninsula]]. The tumulus is an oval-shaped {{nihongo|''empun''-style|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} ''kofun'', with maximal length of 21 meters, partially surrounded by horseshoe-shaped moat three meters wide and 0.5 meters deep. The horizontal [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] opens to the southwest, and has a total length of 9.85 meters, length of 5.80 meters, width of 2.65 meters, and height of 2.76 meters and is the largest in the Hokuriku region. It is made of [[:en:Granite|Granite]] and [[:en:Andesite|Andesite]] and the sarcophagus is made of granite. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
*Major axis: 21meters *Minor axis: 18.5 meters *Height: 3.5 meters
This tomb was excavated by locals in 1903, and a house-shaped [[:en:Sarcophagus|Sarcophagus]] with a roofline decorated with [[:en:Chigi (architecture)|''chigi'']] was found. It is an Ishikawa Prefectural Tangible Cultural Property. [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] included [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], swords, [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirrors]], beads and other jewellery, and horse fittings. The tumulus is believed to have been built in the latter half of the 6th century, based on excavated items.<ref name="Shiseki"/> Restoration work on the tumulus was carried out from 1986 to 1988, and the tumulus was partially dismantled and repaired in 2006 to 2007.
Some of the excavated items are kept at the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]]. The site is open to the public as part of an [[:en:Archaeological park|Archaeological park]]. The burial chamber can be viewed only from the outside. It is located about 20 minutes by car from [[:en:Minami-Hakui Station|Minami-Hakui Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Nanao Line|Nanao Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Sandenkanaya Kofun]][[ja:散田金谷古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Sandenkanaya Kofun]][[ja:散田金谷古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Sandenkanaya Kofun]][[ja:散田金谷古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q18336996]] Wikidata name: Sandenkanaya Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:05, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Sandenkanaya Kofun | native_name = 散田金谷古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Ishikawa Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Sandenkanaya Kofun | map_size = | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|36.855306|N|136.816028|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Hodatsushimizu|WD=Q1204817}} | region = {{ill|Hokuriku region|en|Hokuriku region|WD=Q381025|lt=Hokuriku region}} | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (park) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }}
The {{nihongo|'''Sandenkanaya Kofun'''|散田金谷古墳|}}} is a late [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial tumulus]] located in the Sanden neighborhood of the town of {{ill|Hōdatsushimizu, Ishikawa|en|Hōdatsushimizu, Ishikawa|WD=Q1204817|lt=Hōdatsushimizu, Ishikawa}} in the {{ill|Hokuriku region|en|Hokuriku region|WD=Q381025|lt=Hokuriku region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1982.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138417/9 |title=散田金谷古墳|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Sandenkanaya Kofun is located a hill at the confluence of the Shio River and the Kose River at the base of the {{ill|Noto Peninsula|en|Noto Peninsula|WD=Q691151|lt=Noto Peninsula}}. The tumulus is an oval-shaped {{nihongo|''empun''-style|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} ''kofun'', with maximal length of 21 meters, partially surrounded by horseshoe-shaped moat three meters wide and 0.5 meters deep. The horizontal [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] opens to the southwest, and has a total length of 9.85 meters, length of 5.80 meters, width of 2.65 meters, and height of 2.76 meters and is the largest in the Hokuriku region. It is made of {{ill|Granite|en|Granite|WD=Q41177|lt=Granite}} and {{ill|Andesite|en|Andesite|WD=Q181908|lt=Andesite}} and the sarcophagus is made of granite. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
*Major axis: 21meters *Minor axis: 18.5 meters *Height: 3.5 meters
This tomb was excavated by locals in 1903, and a house-shaped {{ill|Sarcophagus|en|Sarcophagus|WD=Q48634|lt=Sarcophagus}} with a roofline decorated with {{ill|Chigi (architecture)|en|Chigi (architecture)|WD=Q262282|lt=''chigi''}} was found. It is an Ishikawa Prefectural Tangible Cultural Property. {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods}} included {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery}}, swords, [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirrors]], beads and other jewellery, and horse fittings. The tumulus is believed to have been built in the latter half of the 6th century, based on excavated items.<ref name="Shiseki"/> Restoration work on the tumulus was carried out from 1986 to 1988, and the tumulus was partially dismantled and repaired in 2006 to 2007.
Some of the excavated items are kept at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|en|Tokyo National Museum|WD=Q653433|lt=Tokyo National Museum}}. The site is open to the public as part of an [[:en:Archaeological park|Archaeological park]]. The burial chamber can be viewed only from the outside. It is located about 20 minutes by car from {{ill|Minami-Hakui Station|en|Minami-Hakui Station|WD=Q6862870|lt=Minami-Hakui Station}} on the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Nanao Line|en|Nanao Line|WD=Q5357471|lt=Nanao Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ishikawa)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ishikawa)|WD=Q6573264|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ishikawa)}}
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[http://www.pref.ishikawa.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunkazai/siseki/1-5.html Ishikawa Prefectural information home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[http://www.hodatsushimizu.jp/kanko/spot/detail.jsp?id=5486&genre=1 Hōdatsushimizu Information] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.hot-ishikawa.jp/spot/5491Ishikawa Tourist Information] {{in lang|ja}}
{{Kofun navbar}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Sandenkanaya Kofun]][[ja:散田金谷古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Sandenkanaya Kofun]][[ja:散田金谷古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Sandenkanaya Kofun]][[ja:散田金谷古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q18336996]] Wikidata name: Sandenkanaya Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:05, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Sandenkanaya Kofun | native_name = 散田金谷古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Ishikawa Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Sandenkanaya Kofun | map_size = | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|36.855306|N|136.816028|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Hodatsushimizu|WD=Q1204817}} | region = {{ill|Hokuriku region|en|Hokuriku region|WD=Q381025|lt=Hokuriku region}} | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (park) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }}
The {{nihongo|'''Sandenkanaya Kofun'''|散田金谷古墳|}}} is a late [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial tumulus]] located in the Sanden neighborhood of the town of {{ill|Hōdatsushimizu, Ishikawa|en|Hōdatsushimizu, Ishikawa|WD=Q1204817|lt=Hōdatsushimizu, Ishikawa}} in the {{ill|Hokuriku region|en|Hokuriku region|WD=Q381025|lt=Hokuriku region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1982.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138417/9 |title=散田金谷古墳|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Sandenkanaya Kofun is located a hill at the confluence of the Shio River and the Kose River at the base of the {{ill|Noto Peninsula|en|Noto Peninsula|WD=Q691151|lt=Noto Peninsula}}. The tumulus is an oval-shaped {{nihongo|''empun''-style|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} ''kofun'', with maximal length of 21 meters, partially surrounded by horseshoe-shaped moat three meters wide and 0.5 meters deep. The horizontal [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] opens to the southwest, and has a total length of 9.85 meters, length of 5.80 meters, width of 2.65 meters, and height of 2.76 meters and is the largest in the Hokuriku region. It is made of {{ill|Granite|en|Granite|WD=Q41177|lt=Granite}} and {{ill|Andesite|en|Andesite|WD=Q181908|lt=Andesite}} and the sarcophagus is made of granite. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
*Major axis: 21meters *Minor axis: 18.5 meters *Height: 3.5 meters
This tomb was excavated by locals in 1903, and a house-shaped {{ill|Sarcophagus|en|Sarcophagus|WD=Q48634|lt=Sarcophagus}} with a roofline decorated with {{ill|Chigi (architecture)|en|Chigi (architecture)|WD=Q262282|lt=''chigi''}} was found. It is an Ishikawa Prefectural Tangible Cultural Property. {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods}} included {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery}}, swords, [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirrors]], beads and other jewellery, and horse fittings. The tumulus is believed to have been built in the latter half of the 6th century, based on excavated items.<ref name="Shiseki"/> Restoration work on the tumulus was carried out from 1986 to 1988, and the tumulus was partially dismantled and repaired in 2006 to 2007.
Some of the excavated items are kept at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|en|Tokyo National Museum|WD=Q653433|lt=Tokyo National Museum}}. The site is open to the public as part of an [[:en:Archaeological park|Archaeological park]]. The burial chamber can be viewed only from the outside. It is located about 20 minutes by car from {{ill|Minami-Hakui Station|en|Minami-Hakui Station|WD=Q6862870|lt=Minami-Hakui Station}} on the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Nanao Line|en|Nanao Line|WD=Q5357471|lt=Nanao Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ishikawa)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ishikawa)|WD=Q6573264|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ishikawa)}}
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[http://www.pref.ishikawa.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunkazai/siseki/1-5.html Ishikawa Prefectural information home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[http://www.hodatsushimizu.jp/kanko/spot/detail.jsp?id=5486&genre=1 Hōdatsushimizu Information] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.hot-ishikawa.jp/spot/5491Ishikawa Tourist Information] {{in lang|ja}}

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Sandenkanaya Kofun | native_name = 散田金谷古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Ishikawa Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Sandenkanaya Kofun | map_size = | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|36.855306|N|136.816028|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Hodatsushimizu|WD=Q1204817|1=Hōdatsushimizu, Ishikawa}} | region = {{ill|Hokuriku region|en|Hokuriku region|WD=Q381025|lt=Hokuriku region|1=Hokuriku region}} | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (park) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }}
The {{nihongo|'''Sandenkanaya Kofun'''|散田金谷古墳|}}} is a late [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial tumulus]] located in the Sanden neighborhood of the town of {{ill|Hōdatsushimizu, Ishikawa|en|Hōdatsushimizu, Ishikawa|WD=Q1204817|lt=Hōdatsushimizu, Ishikawa|1=Hōdatsushimizu, Ishikawa}} in the {{ill|Hokuriku region|en|Hokuriku region|WD=Q381025|lt=Hokuriku region|1=Hokuriku region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1982.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138417/9 |title=散田金谷古墳|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Sandenkanaya Kofun is located a hill at the confluence of the Shio River and the Kose River at the base of the {{ill|Noto Peninsula|en|Noto Peninsula|WD=Q691151|lt=Noto Peninsula|1=Noto Peninsula}}. The tumulus is an oval-shaped {{nihongo|''empun''-style|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} ''kofun'', with maximal length of 21 meters, partially surrounded by horseshoe-shaped moat three meters wide and 0.5 meters deep. The horizontal [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] opens to the southwest, and has a total length of 9.85 meters, length of 5.80 meters, width of 2.65 meters, and height of 2.76 meters and is the largest in the Hokuriku region. It is made of {{ill|Granite|en|Granite|WD=Q41177|lt=Granite|1=Granite}} and {{ill|Andesite|en|Andesite|WD=Q181908|lt=Andesite|1=Andesite}} and the sarcophagus is made of granite. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
*Major axis: 21meters *Minor axis: 18.5 meters *Height: 3.5 meters
This tomb was excavated by locals in 1903, and a house-shaped {{ill|Sarcophagus|en|Sarcophagus|WD=Q48634|lt=Sarcophagus|1=Sarcophagus}} with a roofline decorated with {{ill|Chigi (architecture)|en|Chigi (architecture)|WD=Q262282|lt=''chigi''|1=Chigi (architecture)}} was found. It is an Ishikawa Prefectural Tangible Cultural Property. {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}} included {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}}, swords, [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirrors]], beads and other jewellery, and horse fittings. The tumulus is believed to have been built in the latter half of the 6th century, based on excavated items.<ref name="Shiseki"/> Restoration work on the tumulus was carried out from 1986 to 1988, and the tumulus was partially dismantled and repaired in 2006 to 2007.
Some of the excavated items are kept at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|en|Tokyo National Museum|WD=Q653433|lt=Tokyo National Museum|1=Tokyo National Museum}}. The site is open to the public as part of an [[:en:Archaeological park|Archaeological park]]. The burial chamber can be viewed only from the outside. It is located about 20 minutes by car from {{ill|Minami-Hakui Station|en|Minami-Hakui Station|WD=Q6862870|lt=Minami-Hakui Station|1=Minami-Hakui Station}} on the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Nanao Line|en|Nanao Line|WD=Q5357471|lt=Nanao Line|1=Nanao Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ishikawa)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ishikawa)|WD=Q6573264|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ishikawa)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ishikawa)}}
